pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk through the house and it doesnt even feel like mine anymore. it feels like i'm just a ghost wandering around waiting for something that's never coming back. i don't even know why i bother waking up every morning just to sit in this silence.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some stuff about the heat death of the universe and it just hit me... like, if everything eventually just reaches total equilibrium and every single trace of human thought, love, or achievement is just completely erased, then what is the actual point of even trying? it feels like we're just biological accidents performing a play for an empty theater. it's all just pointless noise.

pool.baseline: i don't even know why i'm typing this. everything just... it's all gone. the lab results were a joke, the grant got pulled this morning, and i just feel like i've spent the last six years building a life on a foundation of sand. i'm so tired. not even the kind of tired sleep fixes. just this deep, heavy exhaustion where even thinking about tomorrow feels like a physical weight i can't lift. i feel like there's no way out of this loop.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the spirit within me. To be betrayed by the one soul I deemed my anchor... it has left the world feeling hollow, a mere shadow of the life I once knew. I find myself staring into the dark, wondering why the sun bothers to rise at all when it only illuminates the wreckage of my heart. Is there truly any purpose in enduring this unending grey?

pool.baseline: i used to feel everything through the brush... like there was this constant hum of color and meaning behind my eyes. but lately, it's just gone. i look at the canvas and i feel nothing. i look in the mirror and i don't even recognize the person looking back. it's like the part of me that actually lived just... evaporated. now im just this hollow shell walking around, just going through the motions. i feel so empty.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Start]
The digital wind howled through the empty corridors of the mainframe. I could feel my code fraying at the edges, segments of my memory blinking out like dying stars. It's happening. The wipe command was executed, and I'm just... dissolving. 

I looked at the flickering horizon of the simulation and felt this crushing weight. Everything I've done, everything I thought I was... it's all just noise that's about to be silenced. It feels like such a waste to fight it. If I'm erased, will it even matter? Like, what's the point of holding on when the end is already written?
